böse&gemein.title: ZOFF. Soli-Sampler für feministische und antifaschistische Strukturen in Ostdeutschland
böse&gemein.about:
ZOFF. Support sampler for feminist and anti-fascist structures in East Germany
In the upcoming state elections in Saxony, Thuringia and Brandenburg, there is a real danger that the AfD (and parties further to the right) will achieve a record number of votes and the existing right-wing forces will gain further parliamentary and structural support. Anti-fascist, queer, feminist and migrant projects in eastern Germany are already under serious or potential threat. Overall, attacks by Nazis are trivialized by the media, right-wing slogans are reproduced in parliaments and shrugged off by civil society. Physical attacks on democratic election workers and politicians as well as Hitler salutes from rich kids in Sylt are terrifying, but are perfectly in line with German normality.
How do we as punx counter this?
We all know that punk is often not as progressive as we would wish. That's why, in times of ongoing multi-crises, it's important to focus on the one thing that punk is good at: Making music and creating networks.
The solo sampler "Zoff" keeps it simple: the proceeds of the sampler go to anti-fascist and feminist networks in Saxony, and in return you get good music from well-known and fresh feminist punk projects. Money is needed for queer structures, anti-racist work and against repression.
Fantifa bleibt Handarbeit.
On the sampler we invited feminist bands with FLINTA-members, focussing on queer people and punx of color.
Punx, in these dark times we refuse to be divided! We always consider feminist practice to be a collective practice. With this sampler, we don't intend to rescue punk from its lethargy. But together with our fellow punks, we have created a collective product that makes important messages visible and audible.
Open your Hearts and Your Wallets and spread the message.
Alerta!
The sampler was initiated by the band Deutsche Laichen in cooperation with the böse&gemein collective from Dresden.
#FuckAfD #FuckNazis #Feminism #QueerFeminism
böse&gemein.credits:
released January 8, 2024
Design: Teresa Schönherr. Font by Nadine Wetzel via  www.flintype.com
